events.change_summary: Intent check: six architectural problems, multi-signal diagnostics, partial-results visibility, feature parity between modes

events.summary: Intent check pushed — six architectural problems addressed: multi-signal diagnostics, partial-results visibility, feature parity between ASN and country modes, concurrent probing, retry/caching for external calls, and extracted probe engine.

events.change_summary: Full scope: 16 requirements across 3 phases covering all six architectural problems — diagnostic engine, probe failure visibility, typed contracts, structured concurrency, retry reliability, and country-mode parity

events.rationale: Cross-cutting structural refactor touching 6+ modules with new user-visible behavior (multi-signal diagnosis, partial results indicator, country-mode parity). Skip design — all output changes are text additions to existing terminal panels, no layout or visual design decisions required. Include architecture — introduces new system boundaries (types.py, utils.py), a new concurrency model, and a refactored diagnostic engine that spans 3+ subsystems.

alternatives_considered: dataclasses with asdict() serialization — breaks .get() patterns and requires callers to be updated
attrs/pydantic — adds a dependency for no gain over stdlib TypedDict
events.decision: TypedDict over dataclasses for typed data contracts
events.rationale: Hub dicts and MeasurementResult are passed through json.dumps() in --json mode and accessed via .get() throughout callers. Dataclasses would require serialization shims and break existing dict-access patterns across cli.py, mtr.py, and display.py. TypedDict with total=False preserves full backward compatibility while adding mypy/pyright annotation coverage.

alternatives_considered: asyncio with run_in_executor — more complex, no semantic advantage for blocking I/O
multiprocessing.Pool — excessive overhead for short-lived probe calls
events.decision: concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor over asyncio for structured concurrency
events.rationale: All I/O in _measure() is blocking: subprocess.Popen (mtr, traceroute, iperf3, ping), socket operations (Cymru bulk lookup, PMTU ICMP), and requests.get() (RUM, server list). Asyncio would require run_in_executor() wrapping throughout plus an event loop on every call, adding complexity without improving throughput or cancellation semantics. ThreadPoolExecutor context manager provides structured teardown and future cancellation with stdlib only.

alternatives_considered: tenacity — adds a production dependency for functionality achievable in stdlib
urllib3 Retry adapter — couples retry logic to transport layer, harder to apply to non-requests callers (Cymru socket)
events.decision: Local _with_retry helper in utils.py over adding tenacity or urllib3 dependency
events.rationale: The retry requirements are narrow: 3 attempts, exponential backoff, specific exception types (ConnectionError, Timeout, 5xx). A stdlib implementation is ~15 lines, fully testable without mocking third-party internals, and avoids adding a production dependency to a tool that currently has only requests, rich, and typer as runtime deps.

events.summary: Full scope written as revision 2: 16 requirements across 3 implementation phases covering all six architectural problems (diagnostic engine, probe failure visibility, typed contracts, structured concurrency, retry reliability, country-mode parity). Workflow classified as backend-feature / full with architecture stage. Three key decisions logged (TypedDict, ThreadPoolExecutor, local retry helper).

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.change_summary: No open decisions — all major architecture choices settled in scope spec

events.summary: Decisions pass complete — architecture is unambiguous, all major choices (TypedDict contracts, accumulate-all signals, ThreadPoolExecutor) already settled in scope spec; no open decisions require builder input

events.change_summary: Full architecture: accumulate-all diagnostics, probe_errors dict, TypedDict contracts, ThreadPoolExecutor concurrency, _with_retry helper, country-mode parity — 3 phases, 3 new files, 8 modified modules

events.decision: AD-1: Signals format changed from plain strings to {condition, severity, detail} dicts
events.rationale: Structured dicts enable the multi-signal accumulation pattern — the display layer can render sig["detail"] for human output and sig["condition"] for programmatic filtering. Plain strings cannot carry severity metadata needed for worst-severity rollup. test_diagnosis.py assertions updated in Phase 1 alongside the format change to keep the test suite green throughout.

events.decision: AD-2: No gate on path_complete in the accumulate-all diagnostic loop
events.rationale: R1 requires all nine checks to run. In practice, with an incomplete path and no throughput data, only the incomplete-path check fires because bufferbloat/jitter/throughput-cap checks gate on non-None values that won't be present. The existing tests that craft minimal result dicts continue to pass without modification.

events.decision: AD-3: Single ThreadPoolExecutor instance per _measure() call used for all concurrent work
events.rationale: One executor eliminates the coordination overhead of multiple thread pools and ensures all futures are joined when the context manager exits — the structured teardown guarantee that the current bare daemon threads lack. max_workers=8 provides headroom for 4 concurrent independent probes (PMTU, TCP, TLS, RUM) plus the dual-stack pair plus the ping future.

events.decision: AD-4: verdict_panel() signal rendering update moved to Phase 1 alongside the signal format change
events.rationale: Phase 1 changes signals from strings to dicts. Without updating verdict_panel() in the same phase, the terminal display would show raw dict reprs between Phase 1 and Phase 2 completion — a visible regression during development. Scope says Phase 2 adds the partial_results annotation; moving only the signal rendering fix (one line) to Phase 1 keeps each phase's output coherent without expanding Phase 2.

events.summary: Full architecture complete: 3 phases, 3 new files (types.py, utils.py, tests/test_utils.py), 8 modified modules. Key decisions: signals become structured dicts enabling multi-problem accumulation; probe_errors dict replaces scattered _-prefixed error keys; one ThreadPoolExecutor per _measure() call replaces bare daemon threads; verdict_panel signal rendering fix moved into Phase 1 to avoid mid-development display regression.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.note: Starting Phase 1: complete diagnostic reporting and typed contracts

events.details: Baseline was 44 passing. Phase 1 added 3 new tests: test_multiple_simultaneous_signals, test_partial_results_set_when_probe_errors, test_partial_results_false_when_no_probe_errors. All 47 pass; ruff reports zero errors.

events.decision: Implementation: rate_limited_hop signal uses severity "ok" so it never elevates verdict above Healthy
events.rationale: The rate-limited hop case is informational — downstream hops are clean, so there is no end-to-end congestion. Severity "ok" means it accumulates into signals (visible in the panel) but does not influence worst-severity rollup. The top-level verdict remains "Healthy" and _CONDITION_VERDICT["rate_limited_hop"] = "Healthy" confirms this mapping.

events.summary: Replaced early-return pattern in diagnosis.py with accumulate-all signal collection. Added partial_results flag driven by probe_errors. Created types.py with Hub and MeasurementResult TypedDicts. Fixed verdict_panel signal rendering in display.py. Added type annotations to _measure() in cli.py and run()/_parse_traceroute_output() in mtr.py. Updated 6 signal-string assertions in test_diagnosis.py to dict field checks, added 3 new tests. 47/47 tests pass, ruff clean.

events.decision: Implementation: probe_errors dict included in diagnose() return value to supply probe names to verdict_panel()
events.rationale: verdict_panel() only receives the verdict dict (the diagnose() return value). R6 requires "(partial results: {comma-separated probe list})" in the annotation. The probe names live in probe_errors keys. Adding probe_errors to the diagnose() return makes them available at the display layer without changing the verdict_panel() call signature or requiring callers to pass a second argument. The full dict is preserved for JSON output consumers who may want the detail.

Added single retry + warnings.warn to both Cymru lookup functions in asn.py. Updated verdict_panel() in display.py to show partial results annotation with probe names. stageContent.architecture:
### Architecture Summary

The six architectural problems in netpath are fixed in three sequential passes. First, the diagnostic engine is taught to collect every network problem it finds instead of stopping at the first one — a path with bufferbloat, packet loss, and high jitter will now report all three, and the worst one sets the overall verdict. TypedDict contracts are added to make the data shapes visible and verifiable. Second, probe failures become first-class data: every probe that fails records its name and reason in a shared dictionary, the diagnostic engine reads that dictionary and sets a 'partial results' flag, and the terminal output shows a visible annotation so a 'Healthy (partial)' result is clearly distinguished from a fully-verified clean pass. External HTTP calls (server list, Cloudflare RUM) get automatic retry with exponential backoff. Third, the background threads that have no structured cleanup are replaced by a single managed thread pool used for all concurrent work: dual-stack traces, PMTU probing, TCP/TLS latency, and RUM fetching all run in parallel through the same executor, and country-mode sweeps gain the same full probe set that single-ASN mode already provides.

### Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Complete diagnostic reporting and typed contracts

**Verification:**
- [ ] pytest tests/test_diagnosis.py passes with zero failures after signal format change
- [ ] python -c "from netpath.types import Hub, MeasurementResult" exits 0
- [ ] python -c "from netpath.diagnosis import diagnose; r = diagnose({'bufferbloat_ms': 50, 'jitter_ms': 15.0, 'hubs': []}); assert len(r['signals']) >= 2" exits 0
- [ ] python -c "from netpath.diagnosis import diagnose; r = diagnose({'probe_errors': {'iperf3': 'timed out'}}); assert r['partial_results'] is True" exits 0
- [ ] ruff check . reports zero errors

**Estimated effort:** Medium

### Phase 2: Probe failure tracking and external call reliability

**Verification:**
- [ ] pytest tests/test_utils.py passes
- [ ] grep '_trace_error\|_iperf_error\|_speedtest_error' src/netpath/cli.py returns zero matches
- [ ] python -c "from netpath.utils import _with_retry" exits 0
- [ ] ruff check . reports zero errors
- [ ] pytest passes with no regressions from Phase 1

**Estimated effort:** Medium

### Phase 3: Structured concurrency and country-mode parity

**Verification:**
- [ ] grep -n 'threading.Thread' src/netpath/cli.py returns zero results
- [ ] python -c "import concurrent.futures; from netpath.cli import _measure" exits 0
- [ ] pytest passes with no regressions
- [ ] ruff check . reports zero errors
- [ ] manual test: netpath country US --top 2 shows ECMP path count and PMTU in per-ASN output

**Estimated effort:** Medium
stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: Complete diagnostic reporting and typed contracts

**Files changed:** `src/netpath/diagnosis.py`, `src/netpath/display.py`, `src/netpath/cli.py`, `src/netpath/mtr.py`, `tests/test_diagnosis.py`
**Files created:** `src/netpath/types.py`

### What was built

- `diagnosis.py` rewritten: removed all early-return patterns across 9 checks. Every check runs unconditionally and appends `{condition, severity, detail}` dicts to a `signals` list. At the end, worst severity across all signals sets the top-level severity; the highest-severity non-ok signal's condition/detail sets the verdict string and detail. `partial_results: bool` read from `probe_errors` key in the result dict.
- `types.py` created: `Hub` TypedDict (functional form for `Loss%` key) with `total=False`; `MeasurementResult` TypedDict (class form) with `total=False`, covering all public and internal keys including `probe_errors`.
- `display.verdict_panel()` updated: signal rendering changed from `f"  • {sig}"` to `f"  • {sig['detail']}"` to handle the new dict format (per architecture decision AD-4).
- `cli.py`: imports `MeasurementResult`, annotates `_measure()` return type.
- `mtr.py`: imports `Hub`, annotates `run()` and `_parse_traceroute_output()` return types.
- 6 string-based signal assertions in `test_diagnosis.py` updated to `s["condition"]` / `s["detail"]` checks; 3 new tests added: `test_multiple_simultaneous_signals`, `test_partial_results_set_when_probe_errors`, `test_partial_results_false_when_no_probe_errors`.

### No deviations from plan

Rate-limited hop signals use severity "ok" so they appear in the signals list without elevating the verdict — consistent with existing test expectations and the architecture intent.

## Phase 2: Probe failure tracking and external call reliability

**Files changed:** `src/netpath/cli.py`, `src/netpath/diagnosis.py`, `src/netpath/servers.py`, `src/netpath/rum.py`, `src/netpath/asn.py`, `src/netpath/display.py`
**Files created:** `src/netpath/utils.py`, `tests/test_utils.py`

### What was built

- `utils.py` created: `_with_retry(fn, attempts=3, base_delay=1.0)` retries on `requests.ConnectionError`, `requests.Timeout`, and HTTP 5xx responses (detected by duck-typing `status_code` on the return value). Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s. Re-raises last exception on exhaustion.
- `cli.py` updated: replaced `_trace_error`, `_iperf_error`, `_speedtest_error` initial dict keys and all assignment sites with a single `probe_errors: {}` dict. Error keys: `v4_trace`, `iperf3`, `speedtest`. `_run_test()` reads from `probe_errors` dict instead of the removed keys.
- `servers.py` updated: `_fetch_and_resolve()` wraps `requests.get(SERVERS_URL, ...)` with `_with_retry`.
- `rum.py` updated: `fetch_asn_quality()` wraps the `requests.get(...)` call with `_with_retry`; the 401 check and `ValueError` raise remain outside the retry wrapper.
- `asn.py` updated: both `cymru_bulk_lookup()` and `cymru_bulk_lookup_rich()` retry `_cymru_query()` once on `OSError` after a 1s sleep; second failure emits `warnings.warn()` and returns empty dict.
- `diagnosis.py` updated: `probe_errors` dict included in both return paths so `verdict_panel()` can access the probe names for the partial results annotation.
- `display.verdict_panel()` updated: when `partial_results=True`, appends `(partial results: {comma-joined probe names})` to the verdict label.

### One deviation from plan

`probe_errors` was added to the `diagnose()` return dict (in addition to the `partial_results` bool) so that `verdict_panel()` has access to the failed probe names for the annotation. Without this, `verdict_panel()` would only know a partial result occurred, not which probes failed.

architectureIntel.diagram:
flowchart TD
    M["_measure()
cli.py"] -->|"probe_errors dict"| D["diagnose()
diagnosis.py"]
    D -->|"signals list
partial_results bool
verdict/severity/detail"| VP["verdict_panel()
display.py"]
    M -->|"concurrent futures"| EX["ThreadPoolExecutor
v4 trace · v6 trace
pmtu · tcp · tls · rum"]
    U["utils._with_retry()"] -.->|"wraps HTTP calls"| S["servers._fetch_and_resolve()"]
    U -.->|"wraps HTTP calls"| R["rum.fetch_asn_quality()"]
    M --> U

files.description: Remove early-return pattern from all 9 checks. Run every check unconditionally; append matched conditions to a local signals list as {condition, severity, detail} dicts. Derive top-level verdict string and severity from the highest-severity signal. Keep outer try/except Exception safety net; move probe_errors read and partial_results assignment inside the try block. Short-circuit to Healthy only when signals is empty.

Hub TypedDict with fields: count, host, ASN, Loss%, Avg, Best, Wrst, StDev, p50, p95, p99, and optional type. MeasurementResult TypedDict with total=False capturing all public keys returned by _measure() including the new probe_errors key.
In verdict_panel(), change signal rendering from f'  • {sig}' to f'  • {sig["detail"]}' to handle the new dict signal format.
Import MeasurementResult from netpath.types. Annotate _measure() return type as MeasurementResult.
Import Hub from netpath.types. Annotate run() and _parse_traceroute_output() return types.

Update all assertions that check signal list entries as strings to check the dict fields (s['condition'] or s['detail']). Add test_multiple_simultaneous_signals() and test_partial_results_set_when_probe_errors().

phases.scope: Rewrite diagnosis.py to evaluate all nine checks and accumulate every matched condition into a signals list rather than returning on the first match. Each signal becomes a structured dict with condition, severity, and detail fields. Add partial_results to the diagnose() return value. Define Hub and MeasurementResult TypedDicts in a new types.py. Annotate return types in cli.py and mtr.py. Update verdict_panel() signal rendering and test_diagnosis.py assertions to match the new signal format.
phases.title: Complete diagnostic reporting and typed contracts
verification: pytest tests/test_diagnosis.py passes with zero failures after signal format change
python -c "from netpath.types import Hub, MeasurementResult" exits 0
python -c "from netpath.diagnosis import diagnose; r = diagnose({'bufferbloat_ms': 50, 'jitter_ms': 15.0, 'hubs': []}); assert len(r['signals']) >= 2" exits 0
python -c "from netpath.diagnosis import diagnose; r = diagnose({'probe_errors': {'iperf3': 'timed out'}}); assert r['partial_results'] is True" exits 0

files.description: _with_retry(fn: Callable[[], T], attempts: int = 3, base_delay: float = 1.0) -> T. Retries on requests.ConnectionError, requests.Timeout, and HTTP 5xx. Delay sequence: 1s, 2s, 4s. Re-raises last exception on final failure.

Add probe_errors: dict[str, str] = {} to the initial result dict in _measure(). Replace each bare except block that stored a _-prefixed error key with probe_errors['probe_name'] = str(e). Remove _trace_error, _iperf_error, _speedtest_error from initial dict and all assignment sites. In _run_test(), change result.get('_trace_error') to result.get('probe_errors', {}).get('v4_trace').
Import _with_retry from netpath.utils. Wrap the requests.get(SERVERS_URL, ...) call in _fetch_and_resolve() with _with_retry.
Import _with_retry from netpath.utils. Wrap requests.get(...) inside fetch_asn_quality() with _with_retry. Keep the existing ValueError re-raise for 401 outside the retry wrapper.
In cymru_bulk_lookup() and cymru_bulk_lookup_rich(), on OSError retry _cymru_query() once after 1 second. If retry also fails, emit warnings.warn() and return empty dict. Import warnings.
In verdict_panel(), check if verdict dict has partial_results=True and append ' (partial results: {comma-joined probe names from probe_errors keys})' to the label line.

phases.scope: Replace the scattered _-prefixed error keys in _measure() with a unified probe_errors: dict[str, str] populated on failure for each of the six probe types. Introduce _with_retry in a new utils.py and wrap servers._fetch_and_resolve() and rum.fetch_asn_quality() requests.get calls with it. Add single retry plus warnings.warn to the Cymru socket calls in asn.py. Update _run_test() to check probe_errors instead of the removed _trace_error key. Add the partial results annotation to verdict_panel().
phases.title: Probe failure tracking and external call reliability
verification: pytest passes with no regressions from Phase 1

files.description: Add 'import concurrent.futures' at the top. In _measure(), create one ThreadPoolExecutor via context manager wrapping the entire measurement body. In the compare_v6 branch, replace threading.Thread(daemon=True) for v4/v6 with executor.submit(). Collect results via future.result(timeout=...) and populate probe_errors['v4_trace'] or probe_errors['v6_trace'] on TimeoutError. After the trace section, submit pmtu_mod.probe, latency_mod.measure_tcp_connect, latency_mod.measure_tls_handshake, and _fetch_rum as concurrent futures; collect results. Add _run_ping_probe_sync() returning float | None directly; submit it via executor.submit() and cancel after iperf3 completes. In country(), add ecmp_passes=2 and compare_v6=True to both _run_test() call sites.

phases.scope: Replace the two bare daemon threads for dual-stack traces in _measure() with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor managed futures. Replace the background ping daemon thread during iperf3 with a Future submitted to the same executor. Submit PMTU, TCP/TLS latency, and RUM fetch as concurrent futures rather than sequential calls. Add a synchronous _run_ping_probe_sync() for Future use. Update country-mode _run_test() calls to pass ecmp_passes=2 and compare_v6=True.
phases.title: Structured concurrency and country-mode parity
verification: python -c "import concurrent.futures; from netpath.cli import _measure" exits 0
manual test: netpath country US --top 2 shows ECMP path count and PMTU in per-ASN output

architectureIntel.summary: The six architectural problems in netpath are fixed in three sequential passes. First, the diagnostic engine is taught to collect every network problem it finds instead of stopping at the first one — a path with bufferbloat, packet loss, and high jitter will now report all three, and the worst one sets the overall verdict. TypedDict contracts are added to make the data shapes visible and verifiable. Second, probe failures become first-class data: every probe that fails records its name and reason in a shared dictionary, the diagnostic engine reads that dictionary and sets a 'partial results' flag, and the terminal output shows a visible annotation so a 'Healthy (partial)' result is clearly distinguished from a fully-verified clean pass. External HTTP calls (server list, Cloudflare RUM) get automatic retry with exponential backoff. Third, the background threads that have no structured cleanup are replaced by a single managed thread pool used for all concurrent work: dual-stack traces, PMTU probing, TCP/TLS latency, and RUM fetching all run in parallel through the same executor, and country-mode sweeps gain the same full probe set that single-ASN mode already provides.
